Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Ahlens Searches. 36 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Ahlens search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas

A sample of the 60 real customer queries we tested

kosmetika Fiskars sax smycken väska L'Oréal Rituals skonhet hårvård doft hår vård jag behöver något för torr hud Lan hud & hår Lancmoe hudvård hem L'O stora väskor svart L'Oréal smink röd sko parfym hud vård julklappar barn EL svart väska dam L'Oréal Paris
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Ahlens's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 36 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Implement brand and product-type intent boosting before keyword ranking — Fixes Product Discovery by ensuring brand searches like "L'Oréal" and category searches like "parfym" prioritize exact brand matches and product-type relevance over generic keyword scoring.

60%
revenue risk. 36 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
36
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
23
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
1
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
82%
Right products found, ranked too low
#8
Avg. best result position
75%
Best result outside top 3
26%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "mors dag parfym" gets zero results — a blank page, nothing at all.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “parfym” — The highest relevance product 'Fenjal Creme de Parfum Fluid' (0.621 relevance) was buried at position #11, while much lower relevance products dominated the…
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.

Collection routing

Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.

Typo
Typo recovery

Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?

Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 2 friction points, 1 polish note, 1 stable result.
The pattern works in places, but the experience is not consistently clean.
Example “L'Oréal smink röd” — The highest relevance result 'L'Oréal Paris True Match Blush' (0.598) was ranked #7 instead of #1.
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.
Filters & Constraints
Facet extraction
Attribute parsing
3 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “barn skor storlek 25” — The query 'barn skor storlek 25' contains 'storlek 25' (size 25) which should be extracted as a size filter, but the engine treated it as keywords.
Are structured attributes like material, size, or product feature extracted from text?
Next tuning move: Parse attributes into structured facets, then enforce them in ranking or filtering.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

26%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 26% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 26%.

Evidence

  • "skönhet": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "parfym": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "mode": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "hem": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

75%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 45 of 60 queries (75%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "vad har ni för billiga parfymer": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "söker present till min mamma som gillar smink": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "mors dag parfym": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "klädesplagg": #1 was "Dermalogica BioLumin C Serum" (score 0.26). The first result is weakly related to the query.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

7
queries with attribute loss

Result

7 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "barn kläder vinter": dropped barn, kl, der, vinter; partially preserved none
  • "barn skor storlek 25": dropped barn, skor, storlek, 25; partially preserved none
  • "stora väskor svart": dropped stora, skor, svart; partially preserved none
  • "svart väska dam": dropped svart, dam; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

6
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "barn": 6 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "barn": 6 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "hem": 7 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "inredning": 7 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "mode": 8 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Implement brand and product-type intent boosting before keyword ranking

Fixes Product Discovery by ensuring brand searches like "L'Oréal" and category searches like "parfym" prioritize exact brand matches and product-type relevance over generic keyword scoring.

Critical Quick Win
2

Add Swedish language synonym mapping and category recognition

Fixes Language Understanding by mapping Swedish terms like "barn" (children), "skönhet" (beauty), and "kosmetika" (cosmetics) to their corresponding product categories instead of treating them as keywords.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Configure typo tolerance with edit distance algorithms

Fixes Typo Tolerance by recognizing common misspellings like "L'Oréall", "skonhet", and "Lancmoe" and mapping them to the correct brand or category terms.

Critical Medium Effort
4

Implement Swedish natural language query processing

Fixes Language Understanding for conversational queries like "jag behöver något för torr hud" by extracting intent (skincare need) and product type (moisturizer) from natural language patterns.

Critical Major Project
5

Add facet extraction for Swedish size, color, and attribute terms

Fixes Filters & Constraints by recognizing terms like "storlek 25", "röd", and "stora" as filter values rather than keywords, then applying them as product attribute filters.

Critical Medium Effort
6

Configure seasonal and occasion-based query expansion

Fixes Shopping Context by mapping seasonal terms like "mors dag" (Mother's Day) and "sommar rea" (summer sale) to relevant product categories and applying appropriate filters or boosts.

Critical Medium Effort
7

Implement compound word splitting and recognition for Swedish

Fixes Typo Tolerance for merged words like "barnkläder", "hudvård", and "heminredning" by splitting them into component parts and searching for the combined meaning.

Critical Medium Effort
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 25% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #8 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 26% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 vad har ni för billiga parfymer Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search engine returned zero results for the Swedish query 'vad har ni för billiga parfymer' (what cheap perfumes do you have), despite the stats indicating there is a best result at position #16 with relevance score 0.000, suggesting products exist but the engine failed to retrieve any results at all

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#2 söker present till min mamma som gillar smink Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search engine returned zero results for the Swedish query 'söker present till min mamma som gillar smink' (looking for a gift for my mom who likes makeup). The query clearly indicates intent to find makeup products as gifts, but the engine failed to return any results at all.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#3 mors dag parfym Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

The search returned zero results for the query 'mors dag parfym' (Mother's Day perfume in Swedish). Despite the stats indicating a best result at position #16, the actual results shown are empty, meaning customers see no products at all when searching for Mother's Day perfume gifts.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#4 damkläder Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Å WOMAN T-shirt SOFIE' (0.711) was buried at position #15, while the lowest relevance result 'Wera Indoor-jacka JOSEPHINE' (0.566) was ranked #1. Multiple high-relevance women's clothing items like 'Å WOMAN Strumpor i angoramix' (0.684) and 'Å WOMAN Badrock MILOU' (0.660) were pushed down to positions #4, #6, #9, and #12 despite being more relevant than items ranked higher.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.57

Wera Indoor-jacka JOSEPHINE 90 kr

#2 0.61

Slim fit-jeans DNM7 03 120 kr

#3 0.61

Slim fit-jeans DNM7 03 120 kr

#4 0.68

Å WOMAN Strumpor i angoramix 39 kr

#5 0.61

Carin Wester T-shirt SIKA 150 kr

Should have seen

#15 0.71

Å WOMAN T-shirt SOFIE 74 kr

#4 0.68

Å WOMAN Strumpor i angoramix 39 kr

#6 0.66

Å WOMAN Badrock MILOU 299 kr

#9 0.66

Å WOMAN Badrock MILOU 299 kr

#12 0.66

Å WOMAN Badrock MILOU 299 kr

#5 hälsa Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance product 'Rituals Sport 24h Anti-Perspirant Stick' (0.357 relevance) was buried at position #15, while lower-scoring products dominated the top positions. The #1 result 'Rituals The Ritual of Ayurveda - Medium Gift Set 2025' had only 0.348 relevance, and the #2 result 'Pukka Mixask Green Collection Örtte' had just 0.277 relevance. Multiple products with relevance scores above 0.350 were ranked below position #10.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#6 Lancmoe Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine correctly handled the typo 'Lancmoe' and retrieved all Lancôme products, but severely misranked them. The highest relevance result 'Lancôme Renergie Multi-Lift Lifting Firming Anti-Wrinkle Night Cream' (0.609 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-scoring mascara products like 'Lancôme Le 8 Hypnose Mascara Noir Grenat' (0.586 relevance) appeared at #1. Multiple high-scoring products were displaced: 'Lancôme Lash Idole Mascara' (0.594) at #6, 'Lancôme Bi-Facil Lotion Instant Cleanser' (0.594) at #8, and 'Lancôme Rénergie H.C.F. Triple Serum' (0.594) at #13.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.59

Lancôme Le 8 Hypnose Mascara Noir Grenat 238 kr

#2 0.58

Lancôme Le 8 Hypnose Mascara Noir Malachite 238 kr

#3 0.58

Lancôme Hypnose Mascara 465 kr

#4 0.57

Lancôme Definicils Mascara Black 465 kr

Lancôme Hypnôse Mascara Duo Set 600 kr

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"hälsa"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #15

The highest relevance product 'Rituals Sport 24h Anti-Perspirant Stick' (0.357 relevance) was buried at position #15, while lower-scoring products dominated the top positions. The #1 result 'Rituals The Ritual of Ayurveda - Medium Gift Set 2025' had only 0.348 relevance, and the #2 result 'Pukka Mixask Green Collection Örtte' had just 0.277 relevance. Multiple products with relevance scores above 0.350 were ranked below position #10.

Customer Saw

  1. Rituals The Ritual of Ayurveda - Medium Gift Set 2025 (314 kr)
  2. Pukka Mixask Green Collection Örtte (40 kr)
  3. Rituals The Ritual of Hammam Hand Wash (119 kr)
  4. Rituals The Ritual of Sakura Body Lotion Mousse (119 kr)
  5. Rituals Trial Set Homme 2022 (172 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Rituals Sport 24h Anti-Perspirant Stick (97 kr)
  2. Rituals The Ritual of Ayurveda - Large Gift Set 2025 (524 kr)
  3. Rituals Sweet Jasmine Hand Balm (142 kr)

Best match was buried at position #15.

"barn"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #13

The query 'barn' (Swedish for 'children') should map to children's products, but the search engine is treating it as a literal keyword match. The highest relevance result 'Å KIDS Nattlinne PALOMA' (children's nightgown) with 0.516 relevance is buried at position #13, while lower-relevance adult products like 'RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN' (adult bathrobes) with only 0.375 relevance dominate positions #1-5. The engine failed to recognize 'barn' as a category term for children's products.

Customer Saw

  1. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)
  2. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)
  3. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)
  4. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)
  5. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Å KIDS Nattlinne PALOMA (100 kr)
  2. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)
  3. RIKIKI Badrock i frotté TINTIN (249 kr)

Best match was buried at position #13.

"parfym"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #11

The highest relevance product 'Fenjal Creme de Parfum Fluid' (0.621 relevance) was buried at position #11, while much lower relevance products dominated the top positions: 'Rituals The Ritual of Seshen Hair & Body Mist' (0.426 relevance) at #1, 'Sol de Janeiro Brazilian Crush Body Mist Fragrance' (0.516 relevance) at #2, and 'Sol de Janeiro Brazilian Crush Frag Body Mist' (0.494 relevance) at #3. The top 3 average relevance was only 0.479, well below the 0.60 threshold.

Customer Saw

  1. Rituals The Ritual of Seshen Hair & Body Mist (254 kr)
  2. Sol de Janeiro Brazilian Crush Body Mist Fragrance (198 kr)
  3. Sol de Janeiro Brazilian Crush Frag Body Mist (337 kr)
  4. Sol de Janeiro Cheirosa 68 Beija Flor Perfume Mist (198 kr)
  5. HUGO BOSS Boss Bottled Infinite EdP (1040 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Fenjal Creme de Parfum Fluid, 100 ml (97 kr)
  2. Sol de Janeiro Cheriosa 59 Perfume Mist (198 kr)
  3. HUGO BOSS Boss Bottled Infinite EdP (1040 kr)

Best match was buried at position #11.

"mode"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #11

The highest relevance result 'Clique Sverige Bucket Hat' (0.379) was buried at position #11, while much lower relevance items appeared at the top: 'Carin Wester Solglasögon SIGNE' (0.303) at #1 and 'Å WOMAN Stickade fingervantar JULIE' (0.324) at #2. The best result was displaced by 10 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Carin Wester Solglasögon SIGNE (90 kr)
  2. Å WOMAN Stickade fingervantar JULIE (18 kr)
  3. Å WOMAN Keps i bomull ANNA (99 kr)
  4. RIKIKI Stråhatt katt RODNA (45 kr)
  5. Å WOMAN Toteväska GILLIAN (250 kr)

Should Have Seen

  2. Å WOMAN Keps i bomull ANNA (45 kr)
  3. Å WOMAN Keps i bomull ANNA (99 kr)

Best match was buried at position #11.

"hem"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #8

The highest relevance result 'Åhléns Home Bordslampa TOVA' (0.436) was ranked #8 instead of #1, displaced by 7 positions. Lower relevance items like 'Lexington Herringbone Towel' (0.385) and 'Iittala Vitvinsglas' (0.326) were ranked #2, #3, and #7 respectively, pushing better matches down the list.

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"L'Oréall"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #14

Customer searched for 'L'Oréall' (L'Oréal with extra 'l') but got zero L'Oréal products in results. Instead, top results were Biotherm products like 'Biotherm Duo Deo Pure Set' (#1) and 'Biotherm Lait Corporel Body Lotion' (#2), plus some Lancôme items like 'Lancôme Le 8 Hypnose Mascara Noir Grenat' (#8). The best matching result was buried at position #14 with only 0.387 relevance, indicating the search engine completely failed to recognize the typo and match it to the intended L'Oréal brand.

Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 23 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 13 queries ("Estée Lauder", "skönhet", "Estee Lauder"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.
Full Data

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
